about.availabilityText: Available for commissions, residencies, and collaborative projects
about.layersTitle: Work made of layers and time
about.para1: Pilar Olivero (Buenos Aires, 1988) is an artist and art therapist whose practice moves between textile installation, alternative photography, and durational performance. She lives and works in Paris.
about.para2: Trained at the Universidad Nacional de las Artes in Buenos Aires and the École Nationale Supérieure des Beaux-Arts in Paris, her work is grounded in the intersection of body, memory, and archive.
about.practiceTitle: Four ways of touching the world

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Let's talk.
contact.description: Open to commissions, residencies, collaborative projects, and press inquiries. I respond to all messages personally.
contact.intro: Available for collaborations, residencies, and exhibitions.
contact.name: Name
contact.success: Message sent. I'll be in touch soon.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please write directly to

collaborate.label: Contact
collaborate.heading: Shall we work together?
collaborate.description: Open to commissions, residencies, exhibitions, and collaborative projects. Based in Paris, available internationally.

statement.label: Manifesto
statement.text: The body is the first archive. The thread, the first writing. The image, the memory that time could not erase.

textile.title: Textile Art
textile.subtitle: Embroidery · Installation · Weaving
textile.description: The needle as a tool of inscription. Thread as language. Each stitch is a decision, a scar, a mark of presence. Textile work that crosses the boundaries between craft, archive, and political gesture.

photo.title: Alternative Photography
photo.subtitle: Cyanotype · Wet plate · Silver gelatin
photo.description: Light as memory. Obsolete processes reactivated to hold what digital cannot: the grain, the accident, the body's impression on the emulsion. Photography as contact, not capture.

performance.title: Performance
performance.subtitle: Body · Duration · Ritual
performance.description: The body as the primary site of knowledge. Performance as research — slow, durational, often painful. Acts that refuse documentation even as they demand witnesses.

installation.title: Installation
installation.subtitle: In situ · Space · Material
installation.description: Space made porous. Installations that activate rooms, corridors, thresholds — turning architecture into a body and the visitor into a participant. Site-specificity as a condition of meaning.
